questions.text: Which of the following is the symbol for an electric cell?
options: A long line and a shorter thick line
questions.explanation: The long line represents the positive terminal and the short thick line represents the negative terminal.

questions.text: A combination of two or more electric cells is called a:
questions.explanation: Cells are connected positive to negative to form a battery.

questions.text: The thin wire that gives off light in a bulb is called the:
questions.explanation: Filament is usually made of tungsten which has a high melting point.

questions.text: When an electric current flows through a wire, it gets heated. This is the:
questions.explanation: Heat produced depends on the material, length, and thickness of the wire.

questions.text: Which of the following appliances works on the heating effect of current?
questions.explanation: Electric iron uses a heating element to generate heat.

questions.text: A safety device that prevents damage to electrical circuits and fire is:
questions.explanation: A fuse has a low melting point wire that breaks when excessive current flows.

questions.text: MCB stands for:
questions.explanation: MCBs are switches that automatically turn off when current exceeds the safe limit.

questions.text: Hans Christian Oersted discovered that current-carrying wire behaves like a:
questions.explanation: This is called the magnetic effect of electric current.

questions.text: A coil of wire wound around an iron core that acts as a magnet when current flows is:
questions.explanation: Its magnetic strength can be turned on or off with the switch.

questions.text: Electric bells work on the principle of:
questions.explanation: It uses an electromagnet to pull a hammer that strikes a gong.

questions.text: Current flows through a circuit only when the switch is in ______ position.
questions.explanation: ON position creates a closed, complete path for the current.

questions.text: CFL stands for:
questions.explanation: CFLs are more energy-efficient than traditional incandescent bulbs.

questions.text: ISI mark on electrical appliances ensures:
options: Safety and minimum wastage of energy
questions.explanation: ISI is a quality certification by the Bureau of Indian Standards.

questions.text: Electromagnets are used in:
questions.explanation: Their temporary magnetic nature makes them very versatile in technology.

questions.text: When the filament of a bulb breaks, it is called a ______ bulb.
questions.explanation: A fused bulb cannot light up because the circuit is broken.

questions.text: Why is a soft iron core used in an electromagnet?
options: It gains and loses magnetism easily
questions.explanation: Soft iron is a temporary magnetic material.

questions.text: The magnetic effect of current is shown by a ______ placed near a current-carrying wire.
questions.explanation: The compass needle deflects due to the magnetic field generated by the wire.

questions.text: Electric fuses are based on the ______ effect of current.
questions.explanation: Excess heat melts the fuse wire.

questions.text: Heating elements of appliances are usually made of:
questions.explanation: Nichrome has high resistance and a high melting point.

questions.text: Which terminal of a cell is represented by a longer line in a circuit diagram?
questions.explanation: Standard convention for schematic symbols.

questions.text: A wire through which current flows acts as a magnet. This is true:
questions.explanation: Any conducting wire produces a magnetic field when current passes through it.

questions.text: What happens to a compass needle when current in a nearby wire is switched off?
options: It returns to its original north-south position
questions.explanation: The temporary magnetic field disappears.

questions.text: To make a battery of 4 cells, how many terminals are connected to each other?
questions.explanation: Connections are (+ to -), (+ to -), and (+ to -). Total 3 joints.

questions.text: LED stands for:
questions.explanation: LEDs are the most efficient light sources today.

questions.text: Does an electric bulb produce heat along with light?
questions.explanation: Bulbs produce significant heat as a waste byproduct of light generation.

questions.text: Can we use a silver wire as a fuse?
questions.explanation: Fuse wires should have high resistance and low melting point; silver is a great conductor.

questions.text: An electromagnet's strength can be increased by:
options: Increasing number of turns in coil
questions.explanation: All these factors enhance the magnetic field.

questions.text: Circuit diagram is a ______ representation of an electric circuit.
questions.explanation: It uses standard symbols to represent components.

questions.text: Heating of wire depends on its:
questions.explanation: All these contribute to the resistance of the wire.

questions.text: Switch can be placed ______ in the circuit.
questions.explanation: As long as it can break the loop, its position doesn't matter.

questions.text: When the magnetic effect was first observed, what moved?
questions.explanation: The deflection of the needle proved the presence of magnetism.

questions.text: A wire is safe if current is ______ than the fuse limit.
questions.explanation: If current exceeds the limit, the fuse blows.

questions.text: Do magnets have any effect on an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: Like any magnet, it will interact with other magnetic fields.

questions.text: What happens if we reverse the terminals of the cell in a circuit with a compass?
questions.explanation: Reversing current reverses the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.text: Electric geysers use the ______ effect of current.
questions.explanation: They are designed to heat water using electrical energy.

questions.text: In a fuse, the wire used should melt ______.
questions.explanation: Quick melting breaks the circuit promptly in case of a surge.

questions.text: The gong of an electric bell is hit by the:
questions.explanation: The armature is pulled and the attached hammer hits the gong.

questions.text: Current is measured in ______ (basic unit).
questions.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit for electric current.

questions.text: Connecting many devices to one socket can cause:
questions.explanation: Overloading causes excessive heating and may lead to fires.

questions.text: Electric current is the flow of ______ through a conductor.
questions.explanation: In metal wires, current is the drift of free electrons.
